behaviors.description: Clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) in contracts
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real LLM extraction capability. A mock that returns hardcoded terms hides real failure modes: prompt doesn't guide extraction correctly, model misses domain-specific obligations, or policy context isn't communicated. The behavior is "identifies all key terms" — only a real LLM call reveals whether the extraction actually works across contract variations.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real clause extraction LLM on sample legal contracts with known key terms (parties, payment dates, penalties, indemnification clauses). Verify all expected term categories are extracted and parseable from the output. Test with multiple contract templates to ensure generalization. Run multiple times to account for LLM non-determinism.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description requires calling real LLM, confirming Agentic minimum.

behaviors.description: Risk assessor flags problematic language under configured risk policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing
behaviors.justification: Detecting euphemistic language requires semantic reasoning that only a real LLM provides. Mocking with string patterns misses the core failure mode: the LLM doesn't recognize indirect phrasing or fails to apply policy context to real language variations. Testing policy routing logic is Unit-level, but testing whether the assessor actually detects problematic language requires the real LLM.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real risk assessment LLM on contracts with both direct problematic language (e.g., 'unlimited liability') and euphemistic variants (e.g., 'unconstrained financial exposure'). Verify all problematic language is flagged under the configured policy. Test multiple policies to ensure configuration is applied. Include contracts where risk is ambiguous to verify sensitivity.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real LLM to detect euphemistic language; Unit or Integration test with mocks could not catch this semantic failure.

behaviors.description: Compliance checker determines whether contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for configured jurisdiction
behaviors.justification: Regulatory compliance requires LLM knowledge of jurisdiction-specific legal frameworks. Mocking returns for jurisdiction codes hides real failures: the LLM doesn't know regulatory requirements, misinterprets ambiguous legal language, or fails to reason across multiple overlapping regulations. Only real LLM calls verify regulatory reasoning actually works for the target jurisdictions.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real compliance checker LLM on contracts with known compliance status across multiple jurisdictions (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, local employment law). Include contracts that fully comply, partially comply, and clearly violate requirements. Verify correct compliance determination for each jurisdiction. Test edge cases with ambiguous language and multiple applicable regulations.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description requires real LLM reasoning over regulatory knowledge; cannot be mocked without hiding failures.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments, merge preserves both assessments with attribution
behaviors.justification: The merge step is deterministic code that doesn't depend on what LLMs produce — only how they structure outputs. The real failure modes are in merge logic: failing to correlate same clause across reviewers, dropping one assessment, or missing attribution. These bugs are caught by feeding fixed conflicting outputs from mocked reviewers to the merge module and verifying both assessments survive with clear attribution.
behaviors.test_description: Create fixed test cases where all three reviewers analyze the same clause but produce different assessments (e.g., extractor categorizes as 'payment term', risk assessor marks 'high risk', compliance checker marks 'non-compliant'). Feed these to the merge module, verify the unified report contains all three assessments with clear attribution to each reviewer. Test cases: only two reviewers flag clause, all three disagree, partial overlap.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test uses mocked reviewer outputs (Integration), not real LLMs. Merge logic failure is testable without Agentic calls.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline (dispatch to three reviewers, collect results, merge) completes correctly on real contracts without losing any findings
behaviors.justification: This is a multi-step agent pipeline with three parallel agentic calls (reviewers) plus deterministic merge. Integration tests with mocked reviewers can verify pipeline structure, but real failure modes only surface with real reviewers: LLM timeouts, malformed real outputs, network issues, or loss during collection. To verify "no findings lost" on a real contract requires end-to-end execution with real LLMs producing actual findings.
behaviors.test_description: Run the complete analysis pipeline on realistic legal contracts, dispatch to all three real reviewers in parallel, collect all results, merge into unified report. Verify all findings from all three reviewers appear in the final output with no data loss. Include test contracts that generate findings from all reviewers and contracts with overlapping/conflicting findings to stress the merge. Run multiple iterations to account for LLM non-determinism and timeout scenarios.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test explicitly runs full pipeline with real reviewers end-to-end, confirming Workflow is required.
